next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 14

"Path forks," Vel said. "South-southeast. Water first."

The lower route dropped into a settlement built around a well and an eastward lean. Kem saw the lean before he saw the people: three houses listing toward a split in the ground, one already down in a heap of stone and joists. Dren went to the bucket and started hauling with one hand, the bad forearm braced under the rope. The pulley shrieked. Nobody else said anything.

That was how they traveled now. Vel gave directions. Kem answered if an answer was required. Everything she had taught him still lived in his body after trust went bad, which was almost funny if he let himself think about it that way. Thirty-one hummed at the back of his skull, the number she had carried when they met, his now.

Smoke drifted from cookfires on the west side of the settlement. On the east, a fissure ran wide enough to hide its bottom in shadow. Children kept well clear of it, which meant the place had been living with the thing long enough to learn its edges. An old man stacked salvaged boards by a retaining wall. Two women argued about where to move grain before dark.

Then the help call came, high and steady, stripped of panic by repetition. Both of them turned toward the same two-story house at the eastern lip, its upper floor already slanting toward the crack.

Vel set down her pack. Kem did the same. Dren started after them, looked at his arm, and stopped with a muttered curse. He found a low wall instead and said, "I'll keep them back."

A stone-dust man from the crowd was saying, to anyone and no one, "East wall shi...
open_hooks_to_carry: Kem's emergency road-collapse spend (12 levels) and incidentals (7 levels) dropping him from 50 to 31 — Vel's starting number
The cascade mechanism explained: co-spending in proximity stabilizes the threshold; high-to-zero descent amplifies the effect
Vel's manipulation exposed: she needed a high-level spender willing to burn down; Thessa's Ch 10 seed pays off — she's been looking for years
Kem's cold confrontation enhanced with Margins awareness: 'You recruited me as a component. That's what they do here. You replicated the system you claim to oppose.'
Vel's inability to answer because the accusation is accurate — the delay is its own answer
The alliance fractured but intact — they cannot survive alone at these levels
Kem's House signet: taken off his hand, considered for discarding, returned to his pocket

payoffs_to_land.description: Vel's strategic use of Kem is fully exposed — her level-assessment in Ch 3, her spending guidance, her concealment of requirements were all calculated
Vel's private planning about the crossing is revealed to have always included a high-level co-spender as a necessary component
Thessa's Ch 10 revelation pays off: Vel has been searching for a high-level spender for years, making the manipulation premeditated, not opportunistic
The Margins' moral reality contaminates Kem's confrontation — his accusation that Vel replicated the Hoarded's logic of using people gains force from the exploited landscape around them

prev_tail_excerpt:
"Payment was for guide work. Routes, getting you through the Spent Towns and pointed south. That's done."

"Yeah, it's done."

He was quiet for another fifty paces. Vel could hear his breathing, steady, a walker's breath, with a hitch on the left side where his bad arm moved against his ribs.

"I'm not leaving yet," he said.

Vel looked at him. His face was set in the dying light, committed the way a man's face gets after he's turned the same choice over too many times already.

"I'm not asking for more payment," he said. "I'm just — I'm staying on."

"Okay."

"Don't ask me why."

"I won't."

They walked. Behind them Kem was a dark shape against the road. Fifty levels. The number sat in Vel like a cracked tooth.

The notices in her pack rasped against the journal buckle every time she moved. Renewable Expenditure Units. Development Opportunity. Fallow at four in a shirt stitched seven times. The Margins hadn't spent themselves.

Tell him to stop, then. Tell him the rest belongs to the Scour.

Vel kept walking. The words stayed where they'd stuck by the broken wall.

Three days south the basin waited. Maren had marked it. Fallow had named two others. Sera. Hoster. When the flash came back to Vel now, it did not come back alone.

She walked south, and every mile pressed harder.
state_deltas_required: The discovery that Vel engineered the alliance around the cascade mechanism; Kem's betrayal contaminated by the Margins' moral weight
The cascade mechanism revealed; the alliance cracks over Vel's manipulation, now understood through the Margins' lens as systemic rather than just personal; Kem passes through Vel's starting level
